text: Marathon performance is primarily regulated by the rate of aerobic metabolism (marathon VO2) of limited carbohydrate energy stores (muscle glycogen and blood glucose).
The velocity that can be maintained without developing hyperthermia is a key limiting factor in marathon performance.
The theoretical physiological limit for the marathon is approximately 1:58:00, achievable by maintaining current half-marathon world record pace for the full distance.
Running economy and the ability to recruit running musculature in a pattern that minimises fatigue by spreading work over many motor units may represent the ultimate limits to marathon performance.
Carbohydrate availability (muscle glycogen and blood glucose) is a rate-limiting substrate for sustained aerobic metabolism during marathon running.
